annotations.label: Riemannian manifold

anchor.snippet: smooth manifolds with a Riemannian metric

annotations.note: `IsRiemannianManifold I M` is a Prop-valued typeclass on a manifold whose tangent spaces carry a smoothly-varying inner product giving the distance as infimum of path lengths.

annotations.label: Isometry

anchor.snippet: A distance-preserving diffeomorphism between Riemannian manifolds is called an isometry

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Isometry` as any distance-preserving map between (pseudo)emetric spaces, capturing the metric notion but not specifically a diffeomorphism of Riemannian manifolds.

annotations.label: Regular curves are locally isometric

anchor.snippet: Any two regular curves are locally isometric

annotations.note: No result on arclength/local isometry of regular curves was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Theorema Egregium

anchor.snippet: the existence of a local isometry imposes that the Gaussian curvatures at the corresponding points must be the same

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature and its isometry-invariance are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemann curvature tensor

anchor.snippet: the Riemann curvature tensor is an important pointwise invariant associated with a Riemannian manifold

annotations.note: Although covariant derivatives exist (CovariantDerivative), no curvature tensor is defined in Mathlib's manifold library.

annotations.label: Riemannian symmetric spaces

anchor.snippet: An important class of Riemannian manifolds is the Riemannian symmetric spaces

annotations.note: Riemannian symmetric spaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pseudo-Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: the metric tensor need not be positive-definite

annotations.note: Mathlib has no pseudo-Riemannian (indefinite) metric structure on manifolds.

annotations.label: Lorentzian manifold

anchor.snippet: A special case of this is a Lorentzian manifold

annotations.note: Lorentzian manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finsler manifold

anchor.snippet: a Banach norm defined on each tangent space

annotations.note: Finsler structures are only mentioned in a code comment, with no Finsler manifold definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Almost symplectic manifold / symplectic form

anchor.snippet: a smoothly varying non-degenerate skew-symmetric bilinear form on each tangent space

annotations.note: Only the linear-algebra notion of a symplectic (nondegenerate alternating) form is present via SymplecticGroup; no smoothly-varying form on a manifold's tangent bundle is defined.

annotations.label: Symplectic manifold

anchor.snippet: an almost symplectic manifold for which the symplectic form

annotations.note: Symplectic manifolds (closed nondegenerate 2-form) are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectomorphism

anchor.snippet: which preserves the symplectic form is called a symplectomorphism

annotations.note: Symplectomorphisms of manifolds are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectic manifolds have even dimension

anchor.snippet: symplectic manifolds necessarily have even dimension

annotations.note: With no symplectic manifolds defined, this dimension result is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectic manifold in dimension 2

anchor.snippet: a symplectic manifold is just a surface endowed with an area form

annotations.note: No symplectic-manifold/area-form correspondence on surfaces is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Darboux's theorem

anchor.snippet: all symplectic manifolds are locally isomorphic

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Darboux.lean` is the real-analysis intermediate-value theorem for derivatives, not the symplectic Darboux theorem.

annotations.label: Poincaré–Birkhoff theorem

anchor.snippet: if an area preserving map of an annulus twists each boundary component in opposite directions

annotations.note: The Poincaré–Birkhoff fixed-point theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contact structure

anchor.snippet: a smooth hyperplane field H in the tangent bundle

annotations.note: Contact structures (hyperplane fields) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contact form

anchor.snippet: A local 1-form on M is a contact form if the restriction of its exterior derivative to H is a non-degenerate two-form

annotations.note: Contact forms are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Contact Darboux theorem

anchor.snippet: all contact structures on an odd-dimensional manifold are locally isomorphic

annotations.note: The contact Darboux theorem is not in Mathlib (no contact geometry).

annotations.label: Almost complex manifold

anchor.snippet: a vector bundle endomorphism (called an almost complex structure )

annotations.note: Almost complex structures (J with J²=-1 on the tangent bundle) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Almost complex manifolds are even-dimensional

anchor.snippet: an almost complex manifold is even-dimensional

annotations.note: Without almost complex structures, this dimension result is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex manifold

anchor.snippet: An almost complex manifold is called complex if

annotations.note: Mathlib studies manifolds modeled on ℂ via `IsManifold` (e.g. Geometry.Manifold.Complex on holomorphic functions) but has no dedicated complex/integrable-structure definition.

annotations.label: Complex iff holomorphic atlas

anchor.snippet: it admits a holomorphic coordinate atlas

annotations.note: The equivalence of integrable almost complex structures with holomorphic atlases (Newlander–Nirenberg) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Almost Hermitian structure

anchor.snippet: An almost Hermitian structure is given by an almost complex structure J , along with a Riemannian metric g

annotations.note: Almost Hermitian structures on manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kähler manifold

anchor.snippet: a Kähler manifold is a manifold endowed with a Kähler structure

annotations.note: Mathlib's 'Kähler' refers to Kähler differentials (RingTheory) and the 2D kahler form, not Kähler manifolds.

annotations.label: Kähler manifold is complex and symplectic

anchor.snippet: a Kähler manifold is both a complex and a symplectic manifold

annotations.note: Neither Kähler nor symplectic manifolds are formalized, so this relation is absent.

annotations.label: Hodge manifolds

anchor.snippet: the class of Hodge manifolds

annotations.note: Hodge manifolds are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: CR geometry

anchor.snippet: the intrinsic geometry of boundaries of domains in complex manifolds

annotations.note: CR structures/CR geometry are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conformal geometry

anchor.snippet: the set of angle-preserving (conformal) transformations on a space

annotations.note: The angle-preserving (conformal) linear-map notion is formalized as `IsConformalMap`, but conformal structures on manifolds are not.

annotations.label: Differential topology

anchor.snippet: the study of global geometric invariants without a metric or symplectic form

annotations.note: Differential topology is a field rather than a single definition; no corresponding decl exists.

annotations.label: Lie group

anchor.snippet: A Lie group is a group in the category of smooth manifolds

annotations.note: `LieGroup` is a class on a smooth manifold with group structure whose multiplication and inversion are smooth.

annotations.label: Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: the tangent space at the unit endowed with the Lie bracket between left-invariant vector fields

annotations.note: `GroupLieAlgebra I G` is the tangent space at the identity equipped with the bracket coming from invariant vector fields, exactly matching the stated definition.

annotations.label: Geometric analysis

anchor.snippet: a mathematical discipline where tools from differential equations

annotations.note: Geometric analysis is a field, not a single formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Gauge theory

anchor.snippet: the study of connections on vector bundles and principal bundles

annotations.note: Connections (covariant derivatives / Koszul connections) on vector bundles are formalized as `CovariantDerivative`, but principal bundles and gauge theory proper are not.

annotations.label: Theorema egregium (intrinsic invariant)

anchor.snippet: Gaussian curvature is an intrinsic invariant

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature and its intrinsic invariance are not defined in Mathlib.
